title: Update footer to modern clean font style

requirements.summary: Replace the Pacifico cursive font in the Footer component with a modern, clean font family (such as Inter, Poppins, or Montserrat) while keeping the text 'Built And Managed By TixtedEdits' unchanged
acceptanceCriteria: Footer text remains 'Built And Managed By TixtedEdits'
Font is changed from Pacifico to a modern sans-serif font like Inter, Poppins, or Montserrat
Text remains readable and professional looking
Gradient styling and layout are preserved

file_operations.description: Update the Footer component to use a modern sans-serif font (Inter, Poppins, or Montserrat) instead of Pacifico. Change the font-family className from 'font-pacifico' or similar cursive styling to a clean sans-serif font class. Keep the text 'Built And Managed By TixtedEdits', gradient styling, and overall layout unchanged.

Review the Tailwind config's font family definitions. If Pacifico is defined and only used in the footer, consider removing it from the fontFamily configuration. If a modern font like Inter is not already available in the font stack, ensure it is properly configured or use the default sans-serif which typically includes system fonts.

requirements.summary: Remove or update the Pacifico font import from frontend/index.html if it is no longer used elsewhere in the application after the footer font change
acceptanceCriteria: If Pacifico font is only used in footer, remove the Google Fonts import from index.html
If Pacifico is used elsewhere, keep the import but ensure it's not applied to the footer
Page load performance is not negatively impacted by unused font imports

file_operations.description: Review the Pacifico font import link from Google Fonts. After verifying that Pacifico is no longer used in the Footer component or anywhere else in the application (check other components and CSS files), remove the Pacifico font import link to improve page load performance. If Pacifico is still used elsewhere in the app, keep the import but document where it is used.
